steps.title: Turn the wording into two points
steps.content: A fast Desmos setup is to enter the initial value and one next value. The problem gives $(0,70)$. A $50\%$ increase means multiply by $1.50$, so when $x=1$, $y=70(1.50)=105$.

steps.title: Fit the exponential model
steps.content: Use the custom regression $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$. Desmos returns $a=70$ and $b=1.50$, so the equation is $y=70(1.50)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A is the only option with initial value $70$ and multiplier $1.50$. Choice B is decay, and choices C and D start at $50$ instead of $70$, so choice A is correct.
